releases.summary: Major version jump from 0.x to 1.x. Released 3 days ago. Review for API changes before upgrading — both the-squeeze (on 0.460.0) and texas-elections (on 0.575.0) are multiple major versions behind.
Fixes Vite alias resolution in @import and @plugin. texas-elections is on 4.2.1 — minor patch upgrade recommended. the-squeeze is still on Tailwind v3 (3.4.19), a separate migration effort.
Improves canonicalization for tracking-* utilities. Rolled into 4.2.4.

ALERT: Starting April 29 (tomorrow), Hobby plan max log retention capped at 30 days (excludes 10 most recent production deployments and aliased deployments). Pro/Enterprise not affected. Also: new Vercel Flags feature flag product, AI Gateway now has GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, Kimi K2.6, DeepSeek V4, GPT Image 2.

recent_notable.summary: Security release. Fixes CVE-2026-27979 (maxPostponedStateSize DoS via Server Components), CVE-2026-29057 (http-proxy vuln), streaming fetch hangs, and blocks privacy-sensitive dev websockets. All Next.js repos should upgrade. the-squeeze is on 15.x — a major version bump. texas-elections on 16.2.2 — minor bump available.

Node.js 24 is now LTS ('Krypton'). Key upgrades: npm v11 (65% faster installs), OpenSSL 3.5 (security level 2, RSA keys <2048 bits rejected), native TypeScript type-stripping promoted to stable, updated V8 with Float16Array and RegExp.escape(). Tyler's stack targets 22+ — 24 is now the preferred LTS.
Patch release. the-squeeze on 19.2.4, texas-elections on 19.2.3 — minor patch bump available.

Major version with significant breaking changes: ES5 target removed (minimum now ES2015), moduleResolution:classic removed, esModuleInterop/allowSyntheticDefaultImports now always-on, types[] defaults to [] (must explicitly list needed @types/* packages), downlevelIteration deprecated. Run npx @andrewbranch/ts5to6 migration tool. TypeScript 7.0 will be a native Go compiler port. All repos currently on 5.9.3.

urgent.reason: Security fixes CVE-2026-27979 (DoS) and CVE-2026-29057 (http-proxy). All 6 repos have high-severity Next.js vulns in npm audit — upgrade path is available.

recommended: lucide-react 1.11.0 — major version jump from 0.x; review API changes
Node.js 24.15.0 LTS — now preferred LTS over 22; OpenSSL 3.5, npm v11
TypeScript 6.0.3 — major breaking changes; plan migration before 7.0 Go compiler port
fyi: Tailwind 4.2.4 — minor patch; texas-elections can bump in place
Vercel Hobby plan: log retention capped to 30 days starting April 29 (tomorrow)
